name: PT Bank Mandiri (Persero) Tbk
note_net_debt: For banks, net debt is not meaningful in the FCFF sense. Deposits/borrowings are the bank's raw material. Equity valuation via justified P/B is the correct approach.

method_selection.method_why: Bank Mandiri is a single-segment commercial bank (post-BRIS deconsolidation). The only economically correct methods for a bank are: (1) multi-stage DDM capitalizing dividends, (2) excess-returns model (V = BV + PV of excess returns), and (3) the justified P/B identity: P/B = (ROE - g)/(Ke - g). Free cash flow is not meaningful for banks: loans are the product, not capital assets; deposits are not working capital. The CONTRACT registry maps business_type=bank to DDM + justified P/B. The justified P/B is the primary anchor because it maps directly to the crux variable (ROE sustainability) identified by Theia, and it is the universal language of Indonesian bank analysts (1.3x-3.5x P/B), enabling a direct street reconstruction. A single-segment bank does not require SOTP.
method_selection.segment_note: Post-BRIS deconsolidation (effective Jan 2026), BMRI is a standalone commercial bank with five internal business lines (Wholesale, Retail, SME, Treasury, Subsidiaries ex-BRIS). All lines share the same equity capital base, regulatory framework, and cost of equity. No economically distinct segment with materially different risk warrants a separate Ke or a separate method. The P/B identity is applied at the whole-bank level, where the denominator is the consolidated equity and the numerator is sustainable ROE.

risk_free_rate.label: Indonesia 10Y SBN (government bond) yield
risk_free_rate.argument: Indonesian 10-year government bond (SBN) yield approximately 6.7-7.0% in mid-2026. The banking_ddm.py IDR default is 6.8%, consistent with the BI policy rate of 4.75% plus a term premium of ~200-225bp on a 10-year bond. Source: Bank Indonesia press release sp_2727425; ING Think Indonesia rate commentary.

equity_risk_premium.label: Indonesia equity risk premium (ERP)
equity_risk_premium.argument: Damodaran country risk premium for Indonesia: base mature-market ERP ~5.0% plus Indonesia-specific country risk premium ~1.5-2.0% = 6.5-7.0% total ERP. The 6.5% figure is the banking_ddm.py IDR default and reflects Damodaran's July 2025 estimate for Indonesia. IDR weakness in 2026 (17,200/USD) adds macro risk but is partially already in the term premium on the SBN yield. Use 6.5% as the central estimate.
equity_risk_premium.source: Damodaran ERP database; banking_ddm.py IDR defaults

beta.argument: yfinance reported beta = -0.023 (clearly suspect; banking_ddm.py sanitize_beta correctly rejects this as outside [0.3, 2.5] and defaults to EM bank 1.10). Fundamental reasoning: EM large-cap state bank betas typically 0.9-1.3. BCA (BBCA), as the premier private-sector franchise with sticky CASA, warrants beta ~0.9-1.0 (lower governance risk, higher franchise stability). BMRI has two specific risk-elevating factors vs BCA: (a) Danantara governance uncertainty -- directed lending risk, BSI deconsolidation already occurred and more conglomerate restructuring is possible; (b) heavier IDR macro exposure via SOE-linked wholesale book. These add 10-15bp to beta vs BCA, yielding 1.05-1.15 range. 1.10 is the honest center. BMRI's government ownership also adds a structural floor (state would not allow insolvency), which prevents beta from rising above 1.2. 1.10 is not yanked high to manufacture a conservative valuation.
beta.beta_warning: yfinance returned beta=-0.023 (suspect); using fundamental EM bank beta 1.10

ke_build.ke_cross_check: Price-implied Ke at base ROE=20%, g=6%: (20%-6%)/(current P/B 1.38x) + 6% = 16.2%. The gap between 16.2% and 13.95% = 225bp is the macro/governance risk premium the market is currently demanding above fundamental Ke. This is the discount to intrinsic value, not a sign our Ke is too low.

lever1_nim_trajectory.name: NIM trajectory (the margin lever)

base.argument: NIM guidance 4.5-4.7% for FY2026 (revised down from 4.6-4.8% after BRIS decon). Q1 2026 actual NIM 4.70% -- at the top of guidance range. The base assumes NIM settles at 4.6% for the full year as BI rate cuts (expected additional 50bp in 2026 to 4.25% terminal) transmit faster to floating-rate wholesale loan yields than to CASA repricing. This is the honest midpoint: not the bear (full 50bp cut flows into asset yield, compressing to 4.3-4.4%) nor the bull (full cut flows into CoF improvement, holding NIM at 4.7%). Source: BMRI Q4 2025 earnings call; Q1 2026 IDNFinancials; Bank Indonesia press releases.

bull.argument: CoF compression accelerates on CASA (71.6% ratio) repricing faster than asset yields fall. NIM holds 4.7%. Observed: Q1 2026 CoF fell to 1.97% -- already showing CoF benefit.

bear.argument: BI 50bp cut passes fully through floating-rate corporate loans (priced daily vs BI-rate) while time deposits (28.4% of funding) take longer to reprice. NIM falls to 4.3-4.4%.

lever1_nim_trajectory.nim_per_loan_impact: A 10bp NIM change on IDR 1,690T avg loans = IDR 1.69T NII, translating to ~IDR 1.3T net profit (post-tax at 21.5%), or ~IDR 14/share, or ~0.4pp ROE (on IDR 290T avg equity). Confirms NIM is meaningful but not the dominant ROE swing (credit cost normalization is a larger FY2026 driver).

lever2_roe_dupont.name: ROE trajectory -- the crux (DuPont decomposition)
lever2_roe_dupont.dupont_identity: ROE = NPAT / Avg Equity = [NII + Non-II - Opex - Credit Cost] x (1 - tax) / Avg Equity

fy2025_actual.note: FY2025 consolidated including BRIS full year. Post-BRIS standalone is cleaner.

q1_2026_actual_annualized.note: Q1 2026 standalone BMRI ex-BRIS: ROE 22.1% (lower equity base post-decon). Annualized NPAT = Q1 x 4 = IDR 61.5T. This is ABOVE the base case ROE -- confirms base (20%) is honest center, not padded high.

base_fy2026e.note: FY2026E based on management guidance and Q1 trajectory. ROE ~21% for FY2026. For the justified P/B, use steady-state ROE of 20.0% (FY2027+ where credit cost normalizes to midpoint of cycle and the Q1 2026 low-CoC tailwind fades). This is the honest most-likely: Q1 beat reflects low CoC, which is contracted (thick provision buffer) but not expected to persist at 58bp for the full year. 65-70bp FY2026 full-year guidance used.

components.note_reconciliation: This simplified DuPont yields 47T, below the Q1-annualized 61T. The gap arises from uncertainty on non-II (standalone post-BRIS = harder to estimate from consolidated historicals) and equity timing. The ACTUAL anchoring point is Q1 2026 reported NPAT IDR 15.4T (+16.6% YoY, beat by 2.9%), annualized to 61T, which carries higher validity than a reconstructed DuPont. The DuPont is presented to show the lever structure; the absolute NPAT anchor is Q1 2026 actuals.

base_steady_state.argument: Steady-state (FY2027-2028) ROE of 20.0% represents: (a) full-year normalization of the Q1 2026 low-CoC tailwind, (b) NIM settling at 4.6% as the 2026 BI rate cuts fully flow through, (c) continued digital operating leverage (CIR ~41%, gradual improvement), and (d) fee income compounding 8-9% pa from Livin/Kopra. Theia's base projects ROE 19.5% in FY2026 (annualized), 20.0% in FY2027, 20.5% in FY2028. We use 20.0% as the justified P/B anchor (the FY2027 midpoint). This is Theia's sourced base, not a dialed number: it is the honest center of the Q1 actual (22.1%) and the bear floor (17.5%).
base_steady_state.argument_vs_bear: Bear requires NIM at 4.3-4.4% (below guidance midpoint 4.6%) PLUS a lumpy SOE credit event adding 30-40bp to credit costs. Both need to fire simultaneously for ROE to fall to 17-17.5%. The probability of both firing in FY2026-2027 is low given: current gross NPL 0.98% (best in system), provision coverage 271-304x, and no current deterioration signals. The bear is a REAL scenario but not the most likely.
base_steady_state.argument_vs_bull: Bull requires NIM holding 4.7-4.8% (requires full CoF benefit materializing faster than asset yield compression) AND retail loan acceleration to 10%+ (not yet evidenced; wholesale still dominates). Both feasible but not contracted -- correctly placed in the bull, not base.

lever3_growth_g.name: Loan growth and book value growth (g)
lever3_growth_g.system_context: Indonesia system credit growth 9.7% in Dec 2025 (BI data); BI 2026 target 8-12%

base.argument: 8% loan growth is the midpoint of management guidance 7-9% and consistent with system trends. Long-run nominal GDP growth Indonesia: ~5% real + ~3% inflation = ~8% nominal. Terminal g of 6.0% is set at ~75% of nominal GDP, a prudent floor for a bank that is already 22% of the Big Four asset pool -- it cannot sustainably outgrow the economy at its scale. BV growth g = ROE x (1 - payout) = 20% x 32.5% (at 67.5% payout) = 6.5% -- close to the 6.0% terminal g used in the justified P/B.

bull.argument: Infrastructure pipeline (IKN, Prabowo mandate) drives above-system corporate lending.

bear.argument: SOE capex delays + IDR pressure weighs on corporate demand.

lever3_growth_g.duration_note: For a bank, 'duration' maps to: how many years does above-trend loan growth (10%+) persist before normalizing to 7-8%? Theia's evidence suggests 2-3 years of 8-10% growth, then converging to system trend. This is already embedded in the justified P/B terminal g. The growth lever is not the primary swing factor: the ROE lever (how well NIM and credit cost sustain high returns on the growing book) is the crux.

lever4_ke_governance.name: Ke and governance/macro re-rate

base.argument: 13.95% as computed from CAPM. This is the INTRINSIC Ke. The market currently prices a higher effective Ke (~16.2%) due to macro/governance risk premium (IDR at 17,200/USD, JCI foreign outflows, Danantara governance uncertainty). The question is whether this macro premium is EARNED or TEMPORARY. Our judgment: it is temporary -- the business fundamentals are intact (credit quality best-in-system, ROE 20%+, dividend yield 10%), and the macro headwinds are transitory (BI rate path constrained by IDR, not structural deterioration of the bank). Therefore we use the fundamental Ke of 13.95% for intrinsic valuation and reflect the macro discount in #4 (target below intrinsic fair value).
base.macro_premium_at_current_price: 225bp above fundamental Ke = the re-rating opportunity

lever4_ke_governance.re_rate_earned_test: Danantara governance risk is REAL and ongoing (further potential restructuring, directed lending risk). This prevents a full elimination of the macro premium. We credit partial re-rating in #4 (1.60x forward P/B vs justified 1.76x) -- the 225bp macro premium narrows to ~100bp, reflecting the credit quality evidence (CONTRACTED) vs the governance uncertainty (ONGOING OBSERVABLE).
lever4_ke_governance.bull_ke_argument: If Danantara-related governance concerns abate and the IDR stabilizes, the effective Ke could compress toward the peer average (BCA/BRI both trade at implied Ke ~11-12% at current prices). 12.5% is still above the theoretical fundamental Ke, crediting only partial re-rating.
lever4_ke_governance.bear_ke_argument: A significant IDR depreciation event or a Danantara-directed credit loss could push the market's demanded Ke to 15.5%+, consistent with the 2020 COVID trough pricing.

justified_pb_computation.t_invariance_note: For a bank valued on justified P/B, T-invariance is automatic: the multiple is a perpetuity, not a normalization-year discounting. The value is insensitive to the horizon chosen. The DDM triangulation (multi-stage) confirms the same fair value range.

ddm_triangulation.note: Multi-stage DDM run as triangulation cross-check. Inputs: D0 = FY2026E EPS 659 x payout 67.5% = 445 IDR; g_high = 9% (years 1-5); g_declining to g_terminal (years 6-10); g_terminal = 6.0%; Ke = 13.95%.
ddm_triangulation.note_method: Three-stage DDM (5 years high growth, 5 years declining, perpetuity). Consistent with justified P/B at ~5,743 IDR. The ~1.6% difference is within rounding and the transition-stage timing assumption.
ddm_triangulation.cross_check_verdict: DDM and justified P/B agree within 2%. No reconciliation needed.

peer_comps.note: Indonesian bank P/B comps for justified multiple cross-check.

peers.name: BCA (BBCA)
peers.note: Premium franchise (lowest COF, best mobile banking). Private-sector governance. Justified at 3.5x on ROE 22.5%, Ke 12.5-13%, g 6% => (0.225-0.06)/(0.13-0.06) = 2.36x justified vs 3.5x market = BCA has a franchise premium above the Gordon model.

peers.name: BRI (BBRI)
peers.note: Micro/agri specialist. NPL headwinds in 2024-2025. At 1.1x P/B with ROE 18.5%, g=6%: implied Ke 18.7% -- market is demanding high premium for micro credit risk. BMRI deserves to trade above BRI given better asset quality (NPL 0.98% vs BRI's higher micro NPL).

peers.name: BNI (BBNI)
peers.note: Discounted on lower ROE (14.5%) and weaker CASA. At 0.75x P/B with ROE 14.5%, g=6%: implied Ke 15.5%.

peer_comps.verdict: BMRI at 1.38x current forward P/B sits below its justified P/B of 1.76x on a base ROE of 20%. BCA at 3.5x trades at a franchise premium far above its Gordon-justified 2.36x -- private-sector governance and CASA moat command a premium. BRI at 1.1x and BNI at 0.75x trade at discounts reflecting NPL and ROE concerns. BMRI's 1.38x is the deepest discount relative to its Gordon-justified multiple among the Big Four, suggesting the macro/governance risk premium is the dominant pricing driver -- not fundamental ROE deterioration.

at_our_ke_13p95_g_6p0.verdict: The street consensus TP of IDR 5,699 implies ROE of ~19.9% at our Ke of 13.95% and g of 6.0%. This is essentially identical to our base ROE of 20.0%. Our FV of 5,743 vs street mean 5,699 = 0.8% difference. We agree with the street on fundamentals.

at_street_ke_12p5_g_6p0.verdict: If the street is using Ke of 12.5% (a common IDX bank analyst assumption), then the TP implies only ROE 17.4% -- that is more bearish than our base. This means the street may be underwriting a slightly lower ROE with a lower Ke, and the two largely offset.

street_reconstruction.street_thesis: BMRI is Indonesia's largest bank growing loans at 2x system average, running ~20% ROE, paying ~10% dividend yield, at ~1.3-1.5x P/B -- a deep discount to BCA 3-4x P/B -- driven by macro risk (IDR, foreign outflows) rather than fundamental deterioration. The discount closes as rate headwinds ease and credit quality remains robust.
street_reconstruction.our_agreement_disagreement: AGREE on: ROE trajectory (20% base), dividend yield floor, credit quality thesis. DISAGREE on: (a) pace of re-rating (street assumes full convergence to TP, we target partial convergence in 12m given IDR/Danantara overhang); (b) some analysts (Verdhana TP 7,300-8,700) are extrapolating a re-rating to historical P/B highs (~2.5x) which we consider HOPED-FOR rather than contracted. The honest base is 1.76x justified P/B -- not 2.5x.

assumptions.reasoning: CAPM-built from Indonesia 10Y SBN 6.8% + fundamental beta 1.10 x Damodaran ERP 6.5%. Not yanked high to manufacture a conservative valuation. Cross-checked: price-implied Ke at base ROE is 16.2%, confirming a significant macro premium exists above our fundamental Ke.
Above BCA (~0.9) for Danantara governance uncertainty and IDR macro exposure. Below pure cyclicals. Banking_ddm.py EM default is exactly 1.10. yfinance beta of -0.023 is rejected as suspect.

Indonesia nominal GDP ~8% (5% real + 3% inflation). 6.0% is 75% of nominal GDP -- prudent for a mature large-cap bank but arguably could be 7.0% given BV growth = ROE x retention = ~6.5%. The 1pp gap is modest and prevents a g > r blow-up at lower Ke assumptions. One slight low lean, not systemic bias.

Theia base: 19.5% FY2026, 20.0% FY2027, 20.5% FY2028. Q1 2026 actual ROE = 22.1%, annualized NPAT 61T on 290T equity = 21.0%. Using 20.0% as steady-state justified P/B anchor is SLIGHTLY CONSERVATIVE relative to actual run-rate (21.0%) but represents the post-normalization level after credit costs rise from Q1 58bp to FY guidance 60-80bp midpoint. Not padded down.

Midpoint of management guidance 4.5-4.7%. Q1 2026 actual NIM = 4.7% -- at the top of guidance, suggesting the base 4.6% is conservative relative to current actuals. Not padded down; Q1 evidence would support 4.65-4.7% FY2026. Using midpoint is the honest center.
Within guidance 60-80bp. Q1 2026 actual = 58bp (below even the bottom of guidance). Using 65-70bp midpoint for full year normalizes the Q1 beat rather than extrapolating it. Not padded high.

Midpoint of management guidance 65-70%. FY2025 actual was 78% (above guidance range), but management explicitly guided 65-70% for FY2026 as more capital is retained for loan growth. Using guidance midpoint is the honest center.
Q1 2026 actual equity IDR 304.9T / 93.5bn shares = 3,261 IDR. This is the most recent reported actual. No adjustment.

bias_audit.verdict: NO SYSTEMATIC BIAS. One slight lean-low on terminal g (6% vs arguably 7% for Indonesia nominal GDP). All other assumptions sit at their honest center. The base is not reflexively conservative: the ROE assumption (20%) is below the Q1 2026 run-rate (22.1%), which means the base deliberately normalizes the current outperformance rather than extrapolating it. This is honest, not stacked. The CONTRACT §1.3 threshold of 3+ same-direction leans is not breached.

sensitivity_matrix.description: Justified P/B implied price (IDR) at varying ROE (the crux variable) and Ke (the risk lever). Terminal g = 6.0%, BV/share Q1 2026 = 3,261 IDR. This is the mandatory sensitivity over the two levers the call hinges on per CONTRACT §3.
sensitivity_matrix.dominant_lever: ROE -- a 1pp ROE change moves the justified P/B by ~0.13x, worth ~425 IDR/share (13% of current price). Ke is the second lever: a 1pp Ke change moves P/B by ~0.18x at base ROE, worth ~590 IDR/share. Both are material -- neither is second-order at these low-multiple valuations.
sensitivity_matrix.key_observation: Even in the bear scenario (ROE=17.5%, Ke=13.95%), the justified price is 4,717 IDR -- ABOVE the current price of 4,490 IDR. The stock would need BOTH bear ROE AND elevated Ke (14.95%+) to be at intrinsic fair value at the current price. This is the structural BUY case: at current price, the market has priced worse than the bear on fundamentals, or is demanding a 200+ bp governance/macro premium above our fundamental Ke.

scenarios.label: base
scenarios.argument: Theia base: NIM 4.6%, loan growth 8%, credit cost 65-70bp, fee income +8-9% pa. Steady-state ROE 20% reflects the honest most-likely post-normalization outcome. Q1 2026 actual run-rate (ROE 22.1%) shows current performance is above base, which is appropriately not extrapolated into perpetuity.

scenarios.label: bull
scenarios.argument: Theia bull: NIM 4.7-4.8% (CoF compression accelerates), IKN/infrastructure demand sustains 10% loan growth, fee monetization accelerates. Plus: IDR stabilizes, Danantara governance premium dissipates (Ke compresses to 12.95%). ROE 22% is the Theia ceiling (cannot reach BCA-level 24%+ given CASA constraints).
scenarios.note: Bull Ke uses 12.95% reflecting partial macro discount elimination. Bull ROE 22% per Theia bull ceiling.

scenarios.label: bear
scenarios.argument: Theia bear: NIM compresses to 4.3-4.4% as BI cuts flow faster to asset yields; one lumpy SOE credit event adds 30-40bp to credit costs in one year. IDR further weakens (macro risk premium persists, Ke 14.95%). ROE 17.5% per Theia bear. Note: even the bear justified P/B of 1.28x is only marginally below current price (4,179 vs 4,490). The downside is limited at current valuations.
scenarios.note: Bear Ke uses 14.95% reflecting elevated governance/macro risk premium in a stress scenario.

primary_method.name: JustifiedPB
primary_method.reasoning: DDM / Justified P/B is the only economically correct method for a bank (CONTRACT §2.2, METHODS.md registry). Loans are the product, deposits are the raw material, and free cash flow is not a meaningful concept in the FCFF sense. The P/B = (ROE-g)/(Ke-g) identity maps directly to the crux variable (ROE sustainability, as identified by Theia), enables a clean street reconstruction (all Indonesian bank analysts use P/B as the primary anchor), and is robust to the normalization horizon.

justified_multiple.note_pe: Justified P/E derived from justified P/B x (BV/share / EPS) = 1.761 x (3261 / 659) = 8.71x NTM P/E. Current NTM P/E from databundle = 6.62x. The P/E lens confirms the same discount: 8.71x justified vs 6.62x current.

number_1.label: Fair value today
number_1.basis: Justified P/B = (ROE 20.0% - g 6.0%) / (Ke 13.95% - g 6.0%) = 1.761x. Applied to Q1 2026 BV/share 3,261 IDR = 5,743 IDR. The honest base per CONTRACT §1.2: Theia's argued central ROE estimate, CAPM-derived Ke, long-run g anchored to 75% of Indonesia nominal GDP.

number_2.label: Price today
number_2.basis: Market price as of 2026-06-17 (freeze date). Source: market_facts.json.

number_3.label: Fair value in 12m
number_3.basis: #1 rolled forward at Ke 13.95% less FY2026E DPS 445 IDR: 5,743 x 1.1395 - 445 = 6,100 IDR. Pure intrinsic convergence path. DPS = EPS 659 IDR x payout 67.5% = 445 IDR.

number_4.label: 12m target price
number_4.basis: 1.60x forward P/B applied to projected end-2026 BV/share of 3,468 IDR = 5,549 IDR. This is #3 leaned downward by documented judgment: the CONTRACTED evidence (Q1 2026 CoC 58bp below guidance, NIM stable at 4.7%, Q1 beat of 2.9%) supports the base case and justifies pricing above current (1.38x). However the OBSERVABLE macro headwinds (IDR at 17,200/USD, JCI cumulative outflows IDR 61.3T / USD 3.36bn in 2026, Danantara governance uncertainty unresolved) prevent full convergence to intrinsic P/B of 1.76x in 12 months. 1.60x represents partial closure: +22 P/B points above current (1.38x) but -16 points below intrinsic (1.76x). The contracted credit quality evidence moves the target toward fair value; the observable macro does not allow full convergence.
number_4.judgment_documentation: Contracted evidence supporting lean toward fair value: (a) Q1 2026 CoC 58bp -- CONTRACTED per provision coverage 271-304x; (b) NIM 4.7% stable in Q1 -- within guidance; (c) Q1 NPAT beat consensus by 2.9%. Observable evidence preventing full convergence: (a) IDR/USD at 17,200 -- documented foreign outflows; (b) JCI cumulative outflows USD 3.36bn in 2026; (c) Danantara governance transition ongoing. Rating: the contracted evidence is the stronger and more precise factor for ROE, which drives intrinsic value; the macro evidence affects the re-rating timing, not the intrinsic value. Therefore we target 1.60x (partial convergence) not 1.76x (full convergence).

rating.basis: Implied 12m total return to #4 target of 33.5% is comfortably above the 15% BUY threshold. Even the bear intrinsic (4,179 IDR + 445 DPS) implies a near-zero total return, meaning the downside is limited at current prices. The 10.4% dividend yield alone provides a significant return floor. Rating is BUY.

conviction.label: MEDIUM
conviction.reasoning: Valuation confidence is HIGH: the base rests on management-guided and Q1-actual contracted facts (NIM 4.5-4.7%, CoC 60-80bp, loan growth 7-9% -- all management-committed and Q1-actuals within range); band ratio 32.1% (below 35% threshold); data quality CLEAN. Path confidence is LOW: the re-rating catalyst (IDR stabilization, macro risk premium compression, Danantara governance resolution) is genuinely timing-uncertain. The market has been pricing BMRI at a deep discount to intrinsic value for at least 18 months; there is no specific near-term catalyst (BI rate decision, Danantara resolution) that makes re-rating imminent rather than gradual. This is a valuation risk (when does the market close the discount?) rather than a fundamental risk (is the bank's ROE sustainable?). Overall conviction: MEDIUM -- strong valuation case, uncertain re-rating timing.
conviction.weakest_link: Timing of macro/governance discount closure. The fundamental thesis is HIGH confidence. The path thesis is LOW confidence.

sources_cited: BMRI_crux.json (Theia v3) -- ROE trajectory, DuPont decomposition, bull/bear/base scenarios
BMRI_market_facts.json (Pheme) -- price, BV/share, consensus TP, analyst tape
banking_ddm.py -- IDR CAPM defaults, sanitize_beta, justified P/B formula
charter/CONTRACT.md -- §1.3 bias audit, §2.2 bank method, §3 sensitivity mandate
charter/METHODS.md -- bank registry: DDM + justified P/B, P/B duality
